title: 2006 Super League Grand Final
wikitext:
{{Short description|none}} {{Use dmy dates|date=April 2022}} {{Infobox rugby league football match |year = 2006 |title = Super League Grand Final |image = |imagesize = 260px |alt = 2006 Super League logo |home = [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] |away = [[Hull F.C.|Hull]] |home_abbr = ST H {{leagueicon|St Helens|16}} |away_abbr = HUL {{leagueicon|Hull FC|16}} |home_half1 = 10 |home_half2 = 16 |home_total = 26 |away_half1 = 4 |away_half2 = 0 |away_total = 4 |date = 14 October 2006 |stadium = [[Old Trafford (football ground)|Old Trafford]] |location = {{flagicon|ENG}} Manchester |MoM_title = [[Harry Sunderland Trophy]] |MoM = {{flagicon|ENG}} [[Paul Wellens]] ({{leagueicon|St Helens|16}} [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]]) |anthem_title = Headliners |anthem = [[Deacon Blue]] |referee = [[Karl Kirkpatrick]] |attendance = 72,575 |network = [[Sky Sports]] |commentators = [[Eddie Hemmings (rugby league)|Eddie Hemmings]] |commentators2= [[Mike Stephenson]] |commentators3= |tournaments = [[Super League Grand Final]] |last = [[2005 Super League Grand Final|2005]] |next = [[2007 Super League Grand Final|2007]] }} The '''2006 Super League Grand Final''' was the '''9th official Grand Final''' and conclusive and championship-deciding game of [[Super League XI]]. Held on Saturday, 14 October at [[Manchester]]'s [[Old Trafford (football ground)|Old Trafford]] ground, the game was played between [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]], who finished top of the league after the 28 weekly rounds, and [[Hull F.C.|Hull FC]], who finished second after the weekly rounds. ==Background== {{further|Super League XI}} {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;" |- !
